course_subtitle: The Open Compute Project is more than a collection of hardware specifications; it is a global, open source community of engineers, facility operators, and vendors.
course_description_html:
The Open Compute Project is more than a collection of hardware specifications; it is a global, open source community of engineers, facility operators, and vendors.

In this module, you will learn how this ecosystem is governed, how technical decisions are made, and how you can join, consume, or contribute to solve the industry's toughest challenges.

blocks.body_html:
Navigating the OCP Community

The Open Compute Project is more than a collection of hardware specifications; it is a global, open-source community of engineers, facility operators, and vendors. In this module, you will learn how this ecosystem is governed, how technical decisions are made, and how individual projects collaborate to solve the industry's toughest challenges.

blocks.heading_html: By the end of this module, you will be able to:

items_html: Identify the key governing bodies of OCP (The Foundation, Steering Committee, and Project Leads).
Identify the diverse membership tiers and motivations for joining the ecosystem.
Navigate the primary OCP Project Groups and their focus areas.
Understand the lifecycle of an OCP contribution from a draft to a verified Marketplace product.

blocks.body_html: Special thanks to the OCP Steering Committee and our global Project Leads for providing the structural insights and operational data used in this module.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
The OCP vision and strategy span the gamut of data center technology domains, and its success relies on a diverse membership base. As concrete examples to learn from, we'll look at 3 prominent OCP Projects driving impact to the data center landscape.

From hyperscalers mapping out the future of AI to localized vendors building specific cooling parts, the community is vast. And most importantly, this inclusive community is designed for you.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
Organizations and individuals can engage with OCP through various membership tiers.

Whether you are looking to guide global standards as a Platinum member or participate as an individual community engineer, the return on investment is clear.

Active members are eligible to lead projects, author specifications, and contributions that affect their own supply chains, and build crucial partnerships.

Download the attached OCP Membership Benefits document below to explore the specific ROI and advantages of joining the OCP Community.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

lessons.title: The Work of the OCP Community

blocks.body_html:
Having tens of thousands of participants is great, but how does all that diverse talent actually collaborate? Community work happens mostly virtually, accented by major in person events like Global Summits and Tech Days.

Learn more on how the community structures its workstreams to drive innovation. Click play to watch the video.

blocks.quote_html: "Innovation doesn't happen in a vacuum. It happens when you put the hyperscaler who needs the hardware in the same room as the vendor who builds it."

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

items.title: The OCP Foundation & Board of Directors
items.description_html:
The administrative backbone.

The Board of Directors sets the strategic vision, manages the legal framework of open-source intellectual property, and ensures the community remains vendor-neutral and focused on efficiency.

items.title: The Steering Committee (SC)
items.description_html:
The technical gatekeepers. The committee consists of elected industry experts who review all proposed specifications and designs.

They ensure that every contribution meets OCP's strict tenets of Efficiency, Scale, Openness, and Impact before it becomes an official standard.

items.title: Project Groups & Sub-Projects
items.description_html:
The engine room. This is where the actual engineering happens.

Led by volunteer Project Leads, these groups (such as Rack & Power, Cooling Environments, and Hardware Management) draft the specifications, debate the physics, and build the reference designs.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html:
The OCP ecosystem is divided into highly specialized Project Groups, with many of them focusing their expertise on specific hardware elements of the Open Rack frame.

Click the markers on the graphic below to explore how these different workgroups collaborate to build a fully integrated, high density rack.

items.title: (The Chassis/Frame) -> Rack & Power Project:
items.description_html: Focuses on rack architecture, busbar design (like the 48V shift), and blind-mate power delivery.

items.title: (The Manifolds/Pipes) -> Cooling Environments Project:
items.description_html: Develops specifications for advanced thermal management, including Door Heat Exchangers, Cold Plates, and Immersion cooling.

items.title: (The Compute Sled) -> Server Project:
items.description_html: Standardizes motherboard form factors, CPU/GPU integration, and high-performance computing nodes.

items.title: (The Network Switch) -> Networking Project:
items.description_html: Drives open-source network operating systems (SONiC) and vanity-free switch hardware.

items.title: (The Facility/Building) -> Data Center Facility (DCF) Project:
items.description_html: Looks beyond the rack to optimize the physical building, power grid integration, and facility sustainability.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Click on each Marker to learn more.

items.title: Summary
items.description_html: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. In purus velit, tincidunt ac nibh quis, sollicitudin varius libero. Nullam at mi felis. Donec a scelerisque augue, sit amet porttitor nibh. Suspendisse at lorem ut elit placerat blandit.

items.title: Identify & Draft:
items.description_html: One or multiple community members identify an industry challenge and drafts a contribution in the form of a whitepaper, specification, design, etc. within a specific Project or Workstream.

items.title: Community Review:
items.description_html: The Project or Workstream debates, refines, and iterates on the draft, collaborating in the interest of an improved end product while ensuring it aligns with OCP tenets.

items.title: SC Approval:
items.description_html: The finalized draft is presented to the Steering Committee. If it passes their rigorous technical review, it becomes an official OCP Specification.

items.title: The Marketplace:
items.description_html: Vendors build physical products based on the approved specification. Once verified, these products earn the OCP Accepted™ mark and are listed on the OCP Marketplace.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.body_html: Test your knowledge from this module. Select the best answer for each question, then submit.

blocks.question_html: Which OCP body is responsible for the final technical review and approval of all new specifications?
blocks.feedback_correct_html: Correct! The Steering Committee (SC) is responsible for the final technical review and approval of all new specifications
blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite. The Steering Committee (SC) is responsible for the final technical review and approval of all new specifications

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

blocks.question_html: If an engineer wants to contribute a new design for a liquid-cooling blind-mate connector, which Project Group should they join?

choices.text_html: Hardware Management
Cooling Environments

blocks.feedback_incorrect_html: Not quite.

blocks.label: CONTINUE
blocks.complete_hint: Complete the content above before moving on.

items_html: OCP is governed by a clear hierarchy: The Foundation sets the vision, the SC guards the technical standards, and the Projects and Workstreams do the engineering.
Projects and Workstreams are highly specialized, covering everything from the silicon on the server board to the concrete of the facility.
You can engage with OCP through three core pathways: Join the Community, consume from the Contribution Database, or contribute your own solutions.
The OCP Marketplace and Solution Provider Program turns community specifications into real world, purchasable data center products.

buttons.label: explore
buttons.description_html:
View the Complete Project Landscape

Discover the details of the OCP Community structure, including all the top-level Projects, Subprojects and Workstreams on the OCP Community web page.

blocks.body_html: In Module 3, we leave the boardroom and enter the data center. We will deep-dive into OCP Technologies, starting with the physical architecture of the Open Rack v3 (ORv3) and the revolution in liquid cooling.
